title: 2nd Princeton CorpGov Forum Preliminary Speakers for May 21
detail:
2nd Princeton CorpGov Forum Preliminary Speakers for May 21
CorpGov
Mon, March 30, 2026 at 7:00 AM PDT 3 min read
CMTL
MUSQ
GS
BAC

Watch Video Highlights from 1st Princeton CorpGov Forum Below, or Click HERE:

CorpGov will host the second Princeton CorpGov Forum on Thursday, May 21, 2026, at The Nassau Inn in Princeton, New Jersey. The forum, hosted in The Prince William Ballroom, will feature an afternoon of panels, firesides and networking, followed by a reception with cocktails and heavy hors d’oeuvres. ​

REGISTER HERE

Agenda – The Prince William Ballroom:

2pm – Registration and networking

3pm – Panels begin

6pm – Cocktail reception

Panel Topics Include:​

​Governance: Various Stakeholder Perspectives​

Endowment Investments

Capital Markets: Private Equity and IPOs​

Cybersecurity and AI as Boardroom Issues

The Business of Sports and Media

The event will be digitized into a report published on CorpGov and content partners Yahoo Finance, Bloomberg Terminals, Reuters via LSEG Workspace, and AlphaSense, filmed in 4K with professional editing.

We invite institutional investors, corporate executives, and Princeton alumni/students to attend the event. For information on being a speaker, please email Editor@CorpGov.com.

The first Princeton CorpGov Forum was hosted together with The New York Stock Exchange and Goldman Sachs, held on May 22, 2025, at The Nassau Inn in Princeton, New Jersey.

Speakers and Notable Attendees at the Second Princeton CorpGov Forum

Paul Haaga ’70, Former Chairman, Capital Research and Management Company; Chair of the Board, The Ralph M. Parsons Foundation; Board Director, National Museum of Natural History, Smithsonian Institution

Ned Nalle ’76, President, Copper Beaches, Inc.; Previously ION Media Networks, ABC Studios, Universal Studios

Thomas Courtney, Jr. ’86, President and CEO, The Courtney Group

Curtis Glovier ’86, S’87, P’19, P’25, Chief Investment Officer, Star Mountain Capital​

Robert Maciejko ’88, Founder, Board AI Institute; Managing Partner, Oaks Prime Family Office​

John Evans ’91, Co-Founder and Managing Director, Tractus Asia​

Tad Nacheff, Head of East Coast Capital Markets and Financial Sponsor Coverage, NYSE​
